items.title: VI Live: Sporting slaat laat toe en doet goede zaken in titelstrijd
items.summary: Zondag staan er vier wedstrijden in de Eredivisie op het programma. Het duel van Feyenoord volgen we in een aparte live. In deze VI Live blijf je op de hoogte van SC Heerenveen tegen PEC Zwolle, Telstar tegen FC Twente, Sparta Rotterdam tegen NEC én het belangrijkste buitenlandse voetbal.

items.title: Lukkien ziet dat Resink dezelfde stappen kan maken als Valente
items.summary: Dick Lukkien is ervan overtuigd dat Stije Resink toe is aan een volgende stap in zijn loopbaan. De aanvoerder van FC Groningen zag afgelopen transferwindow een overstap naar Benfica afketsen.

items.title: Malen schittert in Napels: Oranje-international grijpt hoofdrol in topper
items.summary: Donyell Malen heeft wederom zijn visitekaartje afgegeven voor AS Roma. Op bezoek bij regerend kampioen Napoli was de Oranje-international twee keer trefzeker. In de slotfase glipte een Romeinse zege alsnog door de vingers.

items.title: 'Zwarte sneeuw' voor Antwerp: 'Dit was niet echt meer een voetbalwedstrijd'
items.summary: In de Eredivisie ging er een streep door het duel tussen Sparta en NEC vanwege sneeuwval, maar in België gingen de duels gewoon door. Antwerp FC ging in eigen huis tegen Westerlo onderuit en dat was niet voor het eerst in de laatste weken.

items.title: Alexander-Arnold bejubeld in Spanje: 'Is Beckham teruggekeerd in Bernabéu?
items.summary: Is nu dan de fase aangebroken waarin Trent Alexander-Arnold met zijn klasse zijn stempel gaat drukken op het spel van Real Madrid? De reacties in Spanje zijn hoopvol na de rentree van de rechtsback in de basis zaterdagavond. Zijn voorzetten en passes tegen Real Sociedad deden denken aan een illustere landgenoot van de 27-jarige Engelsman.

items.title: The curious case of Chermiti in Rohl's Rangers revolution
items.summary: BBC Scotland looks at Youssef Chermiti's impact for Rangers against Hearts and beyond.

items.title: Purpose and togetherness: What the soccer world can learn from FA Cup heroes Macclesfield
items.summary: Macclesfield produced one of soccer's greatest Cinderella stories in beating Crystal Palace in the FA Cup, but the tale behind the triumph is even more remarkable.

items.title: After a dramatic Scottish Premiership weekend, where do we stand now?
items.summary: Four teams in the title race and three points separating the top three - this was a weekend at the top of the Scottish Premiership that swung the race towards a 'race to the wire' contest.

items.title: Arteta: Injury-hit Arsenal 'need some players back'
items.summary: Mikel Arteta admitted a mounting injury list could leave Arsenal vulnerable in their pursuit of silverware this season.

items.title: No notes: FA Cup GK is hero despite research fail
items.summary: Leeds United's Lucas Perri was the penalty shootout hero despite his preparation being foiled by Birmingham City.

items.title: FA Cup 'mud bath' survived by PL strugglers
items.summary: Wolves boss Rob Edwards described his side's 1-0 FA Cup win at Grimsby as an "aggressive game of headers and volleys" as the Premier League club survived a mud bath.

items.title: Macclesfield's Rooney: 'Ambition' to manage higher
items.summary: John Rooney is clear in his ambition to manage at the highest level possible but he wants to go on that journey with Macclesfield.

items.title: De Minaur reigns in Rotterdam | Shelton beats Fritz to Dallas title
items.summary: Alex de Minaur claimed his 11th ATP title after beating Felix Auger-Aliassime in straight sets in the final of the Rotterdam Open.

items.title: The big VAR debate: with, without or change its scope?
items.summary: After a weekend of FA Cup action without VAR, BBC Sport ask managers, fans and pundits whether the game needs technology - and what needs to change.

items.title: 183 team changes in 14 ties - but does it really matter for the FA Cup?
items.summary: Does 183 team changes in 14 ties devalue the FA Cup? Chief football writer Phil McNulty insists the cup retains its old magic.

items.title: Celtic spirit reminds O'Neill of glory years
items.summary: Interim manager Martin O'Neill says the current side's fighting spirit reminds him of his previous Celtic glory years after their comeback win in Kilmarnock.

items.title: Eberechi Eze inspires Arsenal to emphatic FA Cup victory against Wigan
items.summary: It has been a testing few months for the man who scored the winner for Crystal Palace in the FA Cup final last season. But after being substituted at half-time during the disappointing draw with Brentford on Thursday, perhaps this competition could help to breathe new life into Eberechi Eze’s Arsenal career. As well as providing assists for Noni Madueke’s and Gabriel Martinelli’s goals – albeit against a poor Wigan side languishing in the League One relegation zone – the England midfielder’s swagger was back for the first time since he scored a hat‑trick in the north London derby in November. Continue reading...

items.title: Youssef Chermiti hat-trick powers Rangers to victory over leaders Hearts
items.summary: Hearts will take no consolation whatsoever from the fact their progress to the status of serious entity in a Scottish title race was demonstrated by the atmosphere at Ibrox. The scale of celebration that met Rangers’ victory decreed they had not defeated also-rans. Danny Röhl, the Rangers manager, went cavorting down the touchline as his team scored a fourth. This proved the game of the season in Scotland. A genuine thriller. It was also one Rangers dare not lose; that they took three points properly fuels hopes of snatching the league from Celtic’s grasp. An inspired second half from Rangers was sufficient as their visitors wilted. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Super Sunday' - Weston and Stoecker win mixed skeleton gold for Team GB
items.summary: Matt Weston and Tabby Stoecker teamed up to claim Olympic gold in the mixed team skeleton competition.

items.title: 'A throwback' - Wolves beat Grimsby in FA Cup mudbath
items.summary: Premier League Wolves edge League Two Grimsby in an "old school cup tie" which provided an FA Cup "throwback".
